proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative association).
Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with npm1_mutation=0.
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with tp53_mutation=0.
Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 0.231/0.174/0.129 at ecog_ps=0/1/2; univariable logit beta=-0.353, p=1.6e-90.
Logit beta=-0.0049 per 1% marrow blasts, p=2.4e-16.
Logit beta=+0.117 per 1 g/dL albumin, p=2.9e-7.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 have a higher overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 0.220 on vs 0.166 off (delta +0.054); logit beta=+0.346, p=1.0e-50. Strongly supported.
ORR 0.189 on vs 0.186 off (delta +0.003); not significant overall.
ORR 0.186 on vs 0.188 off (delta -0.002); not significant overall.
ORR 0.181 on vs 0.189 off (delta -0.007); p~0.13, not significant overall.
ORR 0.188 on vs 0.187 off; not significant overall.
ORR 0.186 on vs 0.188 off; not significant overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_midostaurin increases probability of objective_response relative to flt3_itd=1 patients not receiving midostaurin (positive interaction treatment_midostaurin x flt3_itd).

Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_gilteritinib increases probability of objective_response relative to flt3_itd=1 patients not receiving gilteritinib (positive interaction treatment_gilteritinib x flt3_itd).

Among patients with idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib increases probability of objective_response relative to idh1_mutation=1 patients not receiving ivosidenib (positive interaction treatment_ivosidenib x idh1_mutation).

Among patients with idh2_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib increases probability of objective_response relative to idh2_mutation=1 patients not receiving enasidenib (positive interaction treatment_enasidenib x idh2_mutation).

analyses.result_summary: Within FLT3-ITD+: ORR 0.176 on midostaurin vs 0.191 off (delta -0.015). Interaction beta=-0.106, p=0.19. NOT supported.
Within FLT3-ITD+: ORR 0.191 on gilteritinib vs 0.189 off (delta +0.002). Interaction beta=+0.079, p=0.32. NOT supported.
Within IDH1+: ORR 0.154 on ivosidenib vs 0.194 off (delta -0.040). Interaction beta=-0.304, p=0.077. Direction is OPPOSITE of predicted.
Within IDH2+: ORR 0.195 on enasidenib vs 0.191 off (delta +0.004). Interaction beta=+0.039, p=0.79. NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 6 treatments x 29 candidate effect modifiers, at least one treatment-by-feature interaction surpasses Bonferroni significance for predicting objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit interaction screen across 6 treatments x 29 features (174 tests) for outcome objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Strongest interactions (sorted by p): treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation beta=+1.055, p=5.5e-102; treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x unfit_for_intensive beta=+0.883, p=1.1e-78; treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x tp53_mutation beta=-0.409, p=4.7e-7; treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x complex_karyotype beta=-0.306, p=6.0e-6; treatment_enasidenib x sex_female beta=+0.314, p=2.2e-4; treatment_7plus3 x complex_karyotype beta=-0.213, p=1.5e-3. Bonferroni threshold ~2.9e-4. Only VEN+AZA-related interactions and possibly enasidenib x sex_female survive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with npm1_mutation=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine markedly increases probability of objective_response relative to npm1_mutation=1 patients not receiving venetoclax_azacitidine (positive interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation).

Among patients with unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine markedly increases probability of objective_response relative to unfit_for_intensive=1 patients not receiving venetoclax_azacitidine (positive interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x unfit_for_intensive).
In patients with npm1_mutation=0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has essentially no effect on objective_response.
In patients with unfit_for_intensive=0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has essentially no effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+: ORR 0.347 on VEN+AZA vs 0.160 off (delta +0.187). Interaction beta=+1.055, p=5.5e-102.
Within unfit_for_intensive=1: ORR 0.308 on VEN+AZA vs 0.163 off (delta +0.145). Interaction beta=+0.883, p=1.1e-78.

analyses.code: Stratified ORR on/off VEN+AZA in NPM1=0 stratum
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1=0: ORR 0.165 on VEN+AZA vs 0.169 off (delta -0.004). Effect essentially absent.

analyses.code: Stratified ORR on/off VEN+AZA in unfit_for_intensive=0 stratum
analyses.result_summary: Within unfit_for_intensive=0: ORR 0.161 on VEN+AZA vs 0.168 off (delta -0.008). Effect essentially absent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, the increase in probability of objective_response is reduced or abolished in patients with tp53_mutation=1 compared with tp53_mutation=0 (negative interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x tp53_mutation).

Among patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, the increase in probability of objective_response is reduced or abolished in patients with complex_karyotype=1 compared with complex_karyotype=0 (negative interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x complex_karyotype).

analyses.result_summary: Within TP53+: ORR 0.164 on VEN+AZA vs 0.167 off (delta -0.004). Within TP53-: ORR 0.226 vs 0.166 (delta +0.060). Interaction beta=-0.409, p=4.7e-7. TP53+ abolishes the VEN+AZA benefit.

Within complex_karyotype=1: ORR 0.175 on VEN+AZA vs 0.163 off (delta +0.012). Within complex_karyotype=0: ORR 0.228 vs 0.167 (delta +0.061). Interaction beta=-0.306, p=6.0e-6. Complex karyotype suppresses the VEN+AZA benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: VEN+AZA increases objective_response only in patients meeting BOTH npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1; outside this joint subgroup the effect is null.

VEN+AZA increases objective_response in the joint subgroup defined by npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this joint subgroup the effect is null.

analyses.code: Stratified ORR on/off VEN+AZA in NPM1+ & unfit; in NPM1+ & fit; in NPM1- & unfit; in NPM1- & fit
analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ & unfit: ORR 0.633 vs 0.160 (delta +0.474). NPM1+ & fit: 0.157 vs 0.160 (delta -0.003). NPM1- & unfit: 0.169 vs 0.165 (delta +0.005). NPM1- & fit: 0.162 vs 0.172 (delta -0.010). Strongly supports joint NPM1+ AND unfit definition.

analyses.code: Stratified ORR on/off VEN+AZA in NPM1+ & unfit & TP53- & ck-
analyses.result_summary: Joint subgroup NPM1+ & unfit & TP53- & ck- (n=4531): ORR 0.786 on VEN+AZA vs 0.158 off (delta +0.628). Outside joint subgroup (n=45469): 0.164 vs 0.167 (delta -0.003). Massive concentration of treatment effect in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, lab values (WBC, blasts, albumin, LDH) and other mutations, the indicator (npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0) still strongly modifies the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response (positive interaction).

analyses.code: Adjusted logit: objective_response ~ trt + joint_sg + trt:joint_sg + age + sex + ecog + secondary_aml + unfit + ck + flt3_itd + flt3_tkd + idh1 + idh2 + npm1 + tp53 + wbc + blasts + albumin + ldh
analyses.result_summary: Interaction (trt x joint_sg) beta=+3.04 (OR ~ 21), p=3.3e-296. Treatment main effect outside joint subgroup beta=-0.017, p=0.51 (null). The 4-feature predicate captures essentially the entire VEN+AZA benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_enasidenib increases probability of objective_response in female patients (sex_female=1) but not in male patients (positive interaction treatment_enasidenib x sex_female).

The treatment_enasidenib x sex_female positive interaction on objective_response persists after adjustment for major prognostic covariates (age, ecog, NPM1, TP53, IDH2, complex karyotype, lab values).

analyses.code: Stratified ORR on/off enasidenib by sex; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: Female: ORR 0.212 on enasidenib vs 0.187 off (delta +0.025). Male: 0.165 on vs 0.188 off (delta -0.023). Interaction beta=+0.314, p=2.2e-4. Effect is small absolute but directional and statistically significant.

analyses.code: Adjusted logit objective_response ~ enasidenib + sex_female + enasidenib:sex_female + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction beta=+0.311, p<0.001. Robust to covariate adjustment, but absolute effect remains modest (~+2-3 percentage points in females).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_7plus3 reduces probability of objective_response in patients with complex_karyotype=1 (negative interaction treatment_7plus3 x complex_karyotype).

After exhaustive interaction screening, no clinically large (absolute risk-difference >0.05) treatment-by-feature interaction exists for treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, or treatment_ivosidenib on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Within complex_karyotype=1: ORR 0.156 on 7+3 vs 0.179 off (delta -0.023). Within complex_karyotype=0: 0.195 vs 0.188 (delta +0.007). Interaction beta=-0.213, p=1.5e-3. Modest but real reduction of 7+3 effect by complex karyotype.

analyses.code: Best modifier per treatment from 16-feature interaction screen
analyses.result_summary: Midostaurin: best p=0.15 (secondary_aml); no significant modifier. Gilteritinib: best p=0.037 (unfit_for_intensive, weak). Ivosidenib: best p=0.028 (npm1_mutation, weak); no positive idh1_mutation interaction (interaction p=0.077, OPPOSITE sign). All absolute effect-modification differences <0.06. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all combinations of binary patient features (size 1-10) used to define subgroups, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is maximized by subgroups containing the predicates npm1_mutation=1, unfit_for_intensive=1, tp53_mutation=0 and complex_karyotype=0.

analyses.code: Exhaustive 3^10 enumeration over 10 binary features (sex, secondary_aml, unfit, ck, flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1, idh2, npm1, tp53), retaining subgroups with n>=200 and >=50 on/off, ranked by ORR(on)-ORR(off)
analyses.result_summary: Top 15 highest-effect subgroups all share the predicates npm1_mutation=1, unfit_for_intensive=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0. Adding additional restrictions (sex_female=1, idh1=0, idh2=0, secondary_aml=0, flt3_itd=0) yields marginally higher deltas (0.665-0.677) but at smaller n. The 4-predicate definition (delta=+0.628 on n=4531) is the parsimonious effective subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL CONSOLIDATED HYPOTHESIS: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine substantially increases the probability of objective_response (absolute risk difference ~+0.63, ORR 0.79 vs 0.16) only in patients meeting all four of: npm1_mutation=1, unfit_for_intensive=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0. Outside this 4-predicate subgroup the treatment has no detectable effect.

FINAL: For treatments other than venetoclax_azacitidine, no large or robust treatment-effect-heterogeneity subgroup exists for objective_response in this cohort. The strongest residual signal is treatment_enasidenib x sex_female=1 (~+2.5 pp boost in females, -2.3 pp in males); modifications by complex_karyotype on treatment_7plus3 are statistically detectable but small in magnitude.

FINAL: On main effects, ecog_ps, blast_pct_marrow, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, and wbc_k_per_ul all decrease objective_response, while npm1_mutation, albumin_g_dl, and unfit_for_intensive (entirely via VEN+AZA channeling) increase objective_response. Age, sex, secondary_aml, FLT3, IDH1/2, and most lab values have no detectable independent main effect.

analyses.code: Final stratified summary of VEN+AZA effect inside vs outside the 4-predicate subgroup; adjusted logit with joint indicator x treatment interaction
analyses.result_summary: Inside joint subgroup (n=4531): ORR 78.6% on VEN+AZA vs 15.8% off, chi2=1766, p<<1e-300. Outside joint subgroup (n=45469): 16.4% vs 16.7%, p=0.51. Adjusted joint x treatment interaction beta=+3.04 (OR~21), p=3.3e-296.

analyses.code: Cross-treatment summary of best interactions and effect sizes
analyses.result_summary: Best non-VEN+AZA interaction: enasidenib x sex_female (beta=+0.31, p=2.2e-4; female delta=+0.025, male delta=-0.023). Next: 7plus3 x complex_karyotype (beta=-0.21, p=1.5e-3; complex_karyotype delta=-0.023). All absolute effect-modification differences <0.05. No subgroup approaches the magnitude of the VEN+AZA signal.

analyses.code: Multivariable logit objective_response ~ all features + all treatments
analyses.result_summary: Significant main effects (Wald p<0.001): ecog_ps beta=-0.361; unfit_for_intensive beta=+0.373; complex_karyotype beta=-0.169; npm1_mutation beta=+0.435; tp53_mutation beta=-0.186; wbc_k_per_ul beta=-0.0019; blast_pct_marrow beta=-0.0051; albumin_g_dl beta=+0.122; treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine beta=+0.358. All other features and treatments p>0.1.
